programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Katey and Dan plan a night of fun with residents Josh and Holly, while Nicolle and Ben take the next step into non-monogamy and try a couple swap. Can Nicolle overcome her anxiety and insecurities?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Luke, whos lost his dating mojo, is raring to reinvigorate his love life and find a lady to relight his fire. And can pole dancer Abi find someone to send her heart into a spin?

programme.title: Sarah Beenys New Country Lives
programme.desc: Dave and Lynn get to work on their dream farmhouse kitchen-diner, while Pete goes shopping for a tractor to cope with his familys new smallholding.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: The fun-filled lives of the Brits seeking a fresh start. In Orlando, its opening night for the Best of British pub. In Kissimmee, Anthonys busy cooking at his own birthday party!

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, the Garners help Helen and Peter chase the Shazza tingle, but will they find it? On the Costa Blanca, the Aldertons deliver some surprises for Caroline and David.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Catherine and Paul have their hearts set on a holiday home to enjoy with their families on the Costa Adeje in Tenerife. Will any of Laura Hamiltons five fantastic properties be the one?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Bob and Vida want to buy a holiday bolthole on the west coast of the Peloponnese that one day could become their permanent home. Can Laura Hamilton find the perfect place for their £300k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Vivienne and Simon reinvent a tired room into an Empire suite. But with loads to do, will the Empire strike back? And panic sets in when Tim and Sashas bride goes missing!

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer take a look back on their biggest builds with complete overhauls, including a higgledy-piggledy layout in Huddersfield to wall-to-wall woes in Woodford.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition is in Guildford, Surrey, where the first host is 22-year-old super-glamorous cosmetic surgery fan Shanice. She hopes to show she is more than just a pretty face by serving up sumptuous food and dazzling conversation.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In and around Guildford, retired estate agent Vivi hopes she can land the thousand pound prize with a Norwegian feast inspired by her homeland.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third day of the competition in and around Guildford and the turn of sales manager Charlotte to host. Charlottes taken a huge risk with her Asian inspired menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the penultimate day of the competition in Guildford, and the turn of childrens entertainments manager, David, to host. Having worked in the entertainment business for the majority of his life, Davids no stranger to showing people a good time.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day in and around Guildford, and marketing and sales director Neil is hoping he can land the prize with his sophisticated menu: a caviar starter, Moroccan lamb main and an artistically angled panna cotta for dessert.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: From the Lakes to Whitby, Paul steams through the north of England on a narrow-gauge loco and a cruise on Windermere, and experiences Queen Victorias private carriage and a 50-tonne wartime loco.

programme.title: Worlds Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: Exploring unique and surprising hotels throughout the world with innovative features and spectacular settings, from cabins dangling from clifftops to clandestine subterranean crash pads.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Dad-to-be Josh, whos 27, has come off his motorbike at speed. Georgie brings in her 10-month-old son Phoenix, whos been sucking on a bleach tablet.

programme.title: Salisbury Poisonings: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The shocking story of the 2018 poisoning of ex-Russian spy Sergei Skripal, his daughter Yulia and Salisbury local Dawn Sturgess. Told through intimate first-hand testimony and with unparalleled access to police, doctors, scientists and the military.

programme.title: Assassin Club
programme.desc: An elite assassin is given his final contract and must kill seven people around the world, only to discover the targets are equally skilled assassins hired to kill him. His only shot to survive is to uncover the mastermind behind the deadly plot.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the Taskmaster in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev Salter and Ian Squire hunt for a man roaming the late-night streets with a machete. The driver of a feisty Fiesta makes off from the cops and takes the pursuit off-road.

programme.title: The Outcast
programme.desc: Jet Cosgrave has been swindled out of his fathers ranch by a wicked uncle. He returns eight years later with a bunch of gunslingers headed by the psychopathic Duke Rankin, and sets about plotting his revenge. Distractions, in the comely shapes of naïve ranch girl Judy Polsen and Alice Austin, his uncles fiancee, cannot deter Jet from the final showdown, but not before the two sides have already shed a lot of blood in dirt.

programme.title: World Seniors Championship Snooker LIVE
programme.desc: Coverage from the invitational seniors tournament which has been played under different formats.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: In Hamilton County, Tennessee police attempt to box in a fleeing car with two suspects inside; in Riverside County, California police pull over a driver with an expired registration.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully investigate the rusting hulk of a new naval destroyer whose crew died of premature ageing. They are reminded of the Philadelphia Experiment, which took place during the Second World War where the US government attempted to make battleships invisible to radar, but stranded on the ghost ship, they also fall prey to a wrinkle in time.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: Conor Woodman discovers the darker side of London when his phone is stolen.

programme.title: Outback Truckers

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.desc: Songwriter Lee Duffy has worked as a performing musician in Benidorm for 15 years. Hes saving money to record his own song in the hope it might be able to feature in an advert or even a film.

programme.title: Bargain Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: After building a successful chain of tattoo studios in Benalmadena, Jackie has plans for expansion. Her plan is to expand her portfolio by going into tea production, but not traditional tea.

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: In Essex, its a grim grime initiation for young apprentice El as shes put to the test, and an ex-cop smells a rat in Buckinghamshire. In Stourbridge, pest control battle with an extreme cockroach infestation.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: With a surge in criminals using fake car number plates to commit crimes, Traffic Cops are under pressure to locate these cloned cars and catch criminals trying to fly under the radar.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Across the UK, 63,000 sexual offences against children and teenagers were recorded. In York, Sergeant Mick leads early morning raids on two men suspected of exploiting and intimidating young teenage girls through the production of images and videos.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A gang have raided more than twenty three cash machines, netting one and a half million pounds.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: An epic cross-country manhunt ends in three arrests and the seizure of a van. On the other side of the county, Ben makes full use of a change in West Yorkshires pursuit policy to drive his car straight into a runaway quad bike.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Profiling the work of high-speed police interception units around the country with some of the most dangerous pursuits and shocking footage ever captured. Exploring what it takes to bring order to chaos.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A dealer is caught stashing drugs in the back of his car. Duncan and Tia search a spooky school for intruders.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: Insurers catch a serial fraudster making fake claims off the back of high-profile tragedies, and theres trouble in store for a supermarket when a group of women stages a fall. (S2 Ep2)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: In the wake of the Coastal News article, tensions remain high between Abigail and Mali. To deal with this, Mali is actively avoiding Abigail.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Beyond frustrated, David pushes Cash for an explanation as to why he chased a fugitive across the country - not to mention dragging Jo along.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: A keen rugby player comes in to the surgery with a blackened toenail hanging by a thread, Nurse Maloney teaches a medical student how to burn lumps of excess flesh off patients bodies, and a pensioners eyelid has frozen leaving him unable to blink.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Sister Benita has just clocked onto her shift in resus and already every bay is taken up with critically ill patients. However, the team must make room as another emergency is being rushed in.

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out
programme.desc: The JRU are tasked with trying to contain a cow on the loose on a road with a 60mph limit. The JRU also respond to reports of a driver unconscious at the wheel of his crashed car.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: The award-winning series returns, following the medical teams at the Royal Stoke University Hospital as they work to save lives. Tino (26) is rushed into hospital after a car crash.

programme.title: Skin AandE
programme.desc: Dr Dev tries to treat a cyst patient with a fear of getting messy. Dr Adils stitching comes under scrutiny from a seamstress. Dr Emma treats a man with a connection to music legends.

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Nightmare neighbours causing chaos across the UK, from noise next door to unwelcome guests, arguments over garden borders and battles for car parking spaces, all unfolding on camera.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The doctors of Balham Park Surgery treat victims of accidents, including a young girl whose hair was ripped out by a whisk, an elderly man who was involved in a hit-and-run, and a woman who fell off her moped.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: During a busy shift in Resus, a 58-year-old woman arrives by ambulance with a dangerously fast heart rate, and a patient comes in with a dangerously slow heart rate following a suspected drug overdose.

programme.desc: When Fox mentions that Hare is his best friend, Owl decides that he needs a best friend, too. One morning, a red balloon suddenly appears near his tree. This is Owls new best friend. They do everything together, but then the balloon slowly starts to deflate.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Pàdruig Morrison reads `An Latha Spòrs written and illustrated by Nick Butterworth and Mick Inkpen, and translated into Gaelic by Jo Macdonald.

programme.title: Strì
programme.desc: Rachel competes against the CBBC ALBA presenters but who will emerge victorious?

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.sub-title: Bhenis a Chinn a Tuath/Venice of the North
programme.desc: Dave and his friends embark on a school trip along the canal where they awaken an ancient monster called Big Lippy.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Dave finds a handheld computer game at the dump. The game involves killing zombies which his friend, Zombie Katie, finds rather insulting. Exacting her revenge, Dave finds himself confronted with a group of very angry and very real zombies.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Zach ri innse mu Theileasgop Sheumais Webb. Astronaut Zach shares some interesting facts about the James Webb Telescope.

programme.title: Hilda
programme.desc: Anns an leabhar-lann, tha an leabhraiche feumach air taic bho Hilda is Flòraidh nuair a tha iad a tighinn tarsainn air Tùr nam Bana-bhuidseach, is nuair a tha aca ri rèiseadh an aghaidh tìm airson leabhar a tha a dhìth a lorg.

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra
programme.desc: Mairi Rodgers explores Scotlands most exotic garden - Logan Botanic Garden, near Stranraer. Tha Màiri Rodgers a gabhail cuairt tro ghàrradh cian-annasach - Gàrradh Lusan Lògan.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.sub-title: Daoine is Dàimhean ----- People and Relationships
programme.desc: Learner Ruth MacAlpine from the Isle of Lewis joins Joy in the studio and Calum Maclean is on the hunt for a world-famous dish which was created in an Indian restaurant in Glasgow.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Mas Fhior
programme.desc: Niall Iain Macdonald hosts the quiz show where two teams go head-to-head to win a cash prize. Niall Iain Dòmhnallach le farpais cheist ùr is dà sgioba a strì airson duais airgid.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Utopia na Smal
programme.desc: Even after the discovery of a radioactive cloud over Sweden, Moscow continues to remain silent. Only 36 hours after the catastrophe does the Soviet government decide to evacuate the contaminated city of Pripyat.

programme.title: Somhairle Spins
programme.desc: Tha slighe dùbhlanach aig Somhairle agus a bhràthair Innes air a chiad latha aca a rothaireachd eadar Inbhir Nis agus Glaschu air an Badger Divide.

programme.title: Alba fon Talamh
programme.desc: Calum starts his underground tour in the north-west of Scotland, exploring the incredible cave networks of Applecross.

programme.title: Fraochy Bay
programme.desc: The local dance troupe are in distress, a situation which calls for careful handling of a musical score.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Ruth MacKay goes fishing and talks about the impact fishing trips have had on her life on the island.

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Drama Gaidhlig steidhichte ann an Leodhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.desc: Sophie looks through her magic binoculars to find out how animals boogie. She learns how different animals dance and, along with her friends, creates a dance using animal movements. Join her and dance like an elephant and a squirrel.

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Tha Linda a tadhal air Meaban is Moo gus dùbhlan a chur romhpa. An-diugh feumaidh rudan òir a lorg. Linda visits Meaban and Moo with a challenge. They must find gold items.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Chan eil Bèibidh Dràgon Brot toilichte oir chan eil cead aige cluich leis an spàin bhrot. The Baby Soup Dragon isnt happy as he cant use the soup spoon.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha rudan do-chreidsinneach a tachairt an-diugh - clobhdan a tha mar thuill. Extraordinary things are happening - cloths that are also holes.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Babs Nicgriogair a leughadh Eanraig agus an t-Inneal. Babs Macgregor reads Eanraig agus an t-Inneal.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.sub-title: Valentin - Am Balach Òg agus an Fhairge
programme.desc: Valentin loves to sail and, in particular, competing with other sailors.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: Tha Sebastian son dearbhadh gur e Belle an cù-chaorach as fheàrr a th ann. Sebastian wants to prove that Belle is the best sheepdog around.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: The Farmer receives a mystery gift from his antipodean cousin that leaves him unimpressed.

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Calum takes a look at the Edinburgh tram network with his cousin, Ruairidh Iain.

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: When the gumbles are struck with boggle twitches, Float is charged with gathering nettles to cure them, but soon finds herself lured in by Weather Snike to cook up a batch for the ailing snikes.

programme.title: Bus Ghlaschú
programme.desc: The story of the Glasgow bus, a lifeline between Donegal and the Scottish city.

programme.title: Immigration Tracks
programme.desc: Anne McAlpine embarks on a cultural voyage of discovery as she picks up the chanter in the Canadian capital, Ottawa, and goes in search of a relative who once farmed cattle in Bolton, Ontario.

programme.title: Am Madadh-Allaidh
programme.desc: The history of the Scottish wolf and its extinction - is it time for their return? Eachdraidh amhadaidh-allaidh an Alba, bho chian a bith. A bheil an t-am aige tilleadh?

programme.title: Dàna: Scotlands Wild Side
programme.desc: Shetlands expansive coastline is home to endless towering cliffs, but its remoteness from the mainland means they see few ascents. With a good friend in tow, Coinneach travels to Shetland in search of new routes and spectacular climbing challenges.

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci - Deisciobal an Eòlais
programme.desc: In Florence, Milan, Rome and finally France, he pours the sum of his scientific and artistic knowledge into a portrait that would become the most famous painting on Earth.

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: In this episode Julie Fowlis and Muireann Nic Amhlaoibh explore their musical roots in Dublin. Singer Darren MacLean and piper Carole-Anne MacKay join our musical hosts for a tour of several iconic venues.

programme.desc: The first round of the quiz for students continues with teams representing the Open University and Newcastle University facing off for a place in round two. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: The Crossworders look for clues as they take on Birkbeck Alumni. The brilliant Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth finds herself faced with one problem after another, all of which seem to centre around her family.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothys handsome cousin Brinsley comes to stay-and supplants Timothy in Mothers affections.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama examines the turbulent years in Britain from 1649 to 1689, from Oliver Cromwells republic to Charles IIs restoration and James IIs subsequent pro-Catholic rule from which he was quickly deposed. This is the dramatic story of the revolutionary period after the execution of Charles I, when Cromwell ruled with an iron hand and Charles II attempted to restore the lustre of the monarchy.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Silvio Berlusconis ambitious takeover of AC Milan reshapes the club and elevates his own profile, charting a path from footballing triumphs to the brink of political power.

programme.title: Saving Venice
programme.desc: Water is the lifeblood of Venice, but it also threatens its foundations. During the pandemic, experts and inhabitants were able to combine their know-how to repair their city and prepare it for the next floods.

programme.title: Scotlands Finest: The Story of the Highland Games
programme.desc: Bill Patterson narrates the story of the Highland Games, recounting a tale of history, pageantry and characters that have shaped a Scottish phenomenon. From the battling clans through to Queen Victorias infatuation with her Highland subjects, the Games have become a symbol of community and identity in Scotland and across the world.

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes travels to China on the trail of Confucius, a great sage of Chinese history whose ideas have fundamentally shaped the country of his birth for around 2,500 years.

programme.title: Lost Cities of The Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Cursed Valley of the Pyramids
programme.desc: A journey to the Lambayeque Valley in northern Peru to find the forgotten ruins of 250 mysterious pyramids, colossal structures made out of mud bricks.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Fake review sites peddling dodgy goods
programme.desc: The team investigate the fraudulent websites convincing shoppers to invest in a best-buy home appliance and meet the woman who learned that her top-rated dehumidifier wasnt up to the job.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is in Wales, where a mixed commercial and residential unit is in surprisingly good condition. Jacquis house in Norwich is also in decent nick, and Dion finds a three-bedroom mid-terrace in Staffordshire with a quirky layout to contend with.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles heads to Sussex, where experts Raj Bisram and Nick Hall join the teams as they search for the best items to sell at auction in Newbury. Eric also sails along the Kennet and Avon Canal to learn about its fascinating 200-year history.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The team try to help a woman in Doncaster who is being scammed while at work, ending up in a tit-for-tat battle with the scammer to persuade her that theyre not part of the scam.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Sea Monster
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough investigates the discovery of a lifetime: the giant skull of a pliosaur. He joins two of the UKs most intrepid fossil hunters on their perilous expedition to excavate the skull from its resting place on a cliff face.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In true Escape to the Country style, todays couple (both former international rugby players) want to leave London in search of a rural idyll in Dorset, and Alistair Appleton is the man to help them on their way. Aside from property, Alistair learns about the health benefits of watercress at a farm which specialises in growing this delicious and nutritious crop.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Flatmates Rachel, Scott, Tara and Daniel take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nicola is forced to come clean to George, Ian makes a rash decision after being humiliated by Oscar, and Linda makes a decision for the sake of her family.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London. They tackle an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charitys ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers before Michelle and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The BBCs Breakfast programme features the latest news, sport, business, weather and other items on a daily basis.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.desc: How much can you trust your MOT? The team investigate the so-called ghost MOTs available for sale and hear from the mum whose second-hand car was far from roadworthy.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy is in Rugeley in Staffordshire to see a three-bed mid-terrace bought by a trio of experienced developers. Martin is in Cudham in the London borough of Bromley, visiting a two-bed end-terrace bought by a mother to help get her daughter on the property ladder. Jacqui is in north west London to see two flats that may be better reverting back to one.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Students take on lecturers in a battle of the brains! In red are friends Amy and Laura, who study history and maths - which they hope will be an ideal combination for bargain hunting! Their opponents in blue are Karen and Nick, who plan to teach the reds a lesson. With pride at stake, both teams dash around an antiques fair in Wetherby in search of three profitable items to sell at auction.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: A man is on the phone to a tech support scammer, leaving the team struggling to find a way to get through to him and stop the scam. And we chat to a fake Jennifer Aniston.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: On the Jurassic coast of Britain a remarkable 200-million-year-old fossil is discovered: the bones of an ichthyosaur, a giant sea dragon. David Attenborough joins the hunt to bring this ancient creatures story to life. Using state-of-the-art imaging technology and CGI, the team reconstruct the skeleton and create the most detailed animation of an ichthyosaur ever made.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The devoted owners of no fewer than six Irish wolfhounds are in desperate need of more space for them and those countless muddy paws, so have set their sights on Suffolk for a bit more elbow room. Briony May Williams is there to guide them as they tour a selection of beautiful homes. Briony also gets to mess about on a stunning million pound, bespoke wooden yacht, a sister vessel to the ones featured in James Bond.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Friends Jordan, Allan, Archie and Lewis take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they must cross the Bridge, stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

Jacqui is in north west London to see two flats that may be better reverting back to one.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Sally Magnusson: Alzheimers, a Cure and Me
programme.desc: Sally Magnusson and her family cared for their mother for many years before her death from dementia. With a possible familial link, Sally has often wondered: Will I be next? Now a cure for Alzheimers is in sight.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: While the Foulkeses celebrate their nuptials, Alex is devastated by a gruesome discovery. Gillian is desperate to explain herself to a furious DCI Douglas after the shock revelation, and Mack takes matters into his own hands to rein Callum in.

programme.title: Jules and Gregs Wild Swim
programme.desc: The crystal-clear ocean waters and impressive beaches of Mull and Iona are the backdrop for Jules and Gregs last adventure of the season.

programme.title: Scotlands Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Judith Ralston and Grado sample Scotlands south coast in Dumfries and Galloway. The adventure starts at the historic Caerlaverock Estate, where Grado enjoys an overnight in a treehouse. They experience majestic birds of prey on the Galloway Red Kite Trail and spend the night in coastal glamping domes. Their final stop takes them to Portpatrick, where they stay in the Edwardian luxury of Dunskey Castle.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Suriname, a country with more forest than anywhere else on Earth. He kayaks upriver in search of some deadly wildlife.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads down in a deep-sea submersible to explore the ocean depths. He then travels to the Natural History Museum to meet some of the weirdest animals from the deep.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: At the halfway stage of the race, the teams discover their next checkpoint lies within Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan. To reach it, they must navigate the endless horizons of the vast Kazakh steppe.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Charles prepares for his very first second date, but he worries he isnt good enough for his suitor. Liv meets a man at the beach for her first ever date.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Olivia invites her date to meet the family. Dianne is happy with how her first date ends. Liv meets a young man for a second date, and they share a passionate moment. Charles continues his search for love, meeting a new man for a teahouse date.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London. They tackle an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charitys ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers before Michelle and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces.

programme.title: Kingdom
programme.desc: Four rival families - leopards, hyena, wild dogs and lions - all try to claim a rich corner of Zambian paradise as their home. Who will succeed in ruling this precious kingdom?

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: In Senegal, west Africa, lives a group of chimpanzees led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for three years, a time when leaders here are usually overthrown, and he finds himself surrounded by rivals who all want his crown.

programme.title: Dynasties II
programme.desc: A mother puma must battle rivals, tackle prey nearly three times her size and endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in her bid to raise four cubs, her biggest litter yet.

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: Tropical rainforests are plant battlegrounds. New filming techniques allow us to enter the world of plants to see it from their perspective and on their timescale.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: Today, more than two-thirds of mammal species are nocturnal, and in this episode we will uncover the intriguing strategies they have evolved, from remarkable senses to teamwork, intelligence and adaptability, which have enabled them to overcome a time we find so challenging. Nocturnal mammals have developed an array of sharp senses. And, unlike us, many are blessed with incredible night vision.

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce, and Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael explores the history of Hokkaidos indigenous Ainu people. At Lake Shikotsu, he enjoys a traditional meal of salmon soup and witnesses a performance of the ancient Dance of the Cranes.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Fly-Tippers Get Caught in the Act
programme.desc: Farmers confront criminals, wildfires rage and hidden horrors emerge on a Welsh farm.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The freedivers take on a search at a castles jetty on Windermere, where five years ago a devastated couple lost their treasured wedding ring. Social media detective Mui gets creative in the hope of tracking down the owner of a phone found in the depths, and our crafter Lucy turns a medley of old bottles and jars pulled out of the lake into something quite magical.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Features and discussions reflect on the lives of individuals with various degrees of hearing impairments. Along the way, the presenters share their perspectives on relevant matters.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clive Myries Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels 200 miles east of Havana to the historic town of Trinidad, a Unesco world heritage site. Along the way, Clive meets the octogenarian carpenter whose skills have contributed to keeping this 16th-century city standing.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers follow lions, leopards and cheetahs in Botswanas Okavango Delta.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: This programme comes from Mellerstain House in the Scottish Borders, where interesting finds include a valuable majolica dish, a dressing case, and a Lowry doodle.

programme.desc: Members of the public are invited to try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michaels South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the worlds largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsungs headquarters.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotlands Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the upper reaches of the river Dee, which rises higher than any river in the UK. In the heart of the Cairngorm mountains, he learns about writer and mountain lover Nan Shepherds passion for the living landscape.

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation episode of the wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough, visiting animals in their habitats across the globe, recorded in stunning ultra-high definition.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: A quiz which compensates for the level of difficulty by awarding points, not just for correct answers but also for `interesting answers, regardless of whether or not they even have anything to do with the original question asked.

programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper, with no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record, concocts an elaborate extortion plan.

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: A fleet of sunken ships that point to ghosts, cannibals, and bloodshed in early America; strange arctic holes that NASA cant explain; near Area 51, mysterious alien structures straight out of Star Wars.

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments

programme.desc: Sula and Amma surprise Bing to show him Sulas sparkly painted butterfly face. Bing also wants a painted face, one that looks like Biteysaurus, and Sula says she can do it for him. programme.desc: Blueys Fathers Day card has to be perfect but she cant decide what to draw. Mum reminds her about all the fun times she has had with Dad.

But Sula and Amma bring him a carroty bagel to wish him a speedy recovery.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: No, Rebecca isnt wearing a box on her head - shes dressed as an astronaut! This gives Murphy an idea. Hes going to help Rebecca discover alien life by creating an alien out of a sock. The Recycling Express and the Tiny Helpers support Rebecca and Murphy as they reach for the stars.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger joins the baby Pea-Rexes on their expedition to find dinner and ends up eating something too hot to handle, which alerts the Bananaraptors.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Mum play Keepy-Uppy around the house with their last balloon, but it becomes competitive when Dad joins in.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are having fun playing games but are surprised to find out that Betty didnt always enjoy playing. Since her first day at Squirrel Club, she has always impressed everyone with her confidence and independence, but because she had grown up playing alone, she didnt know how to join in with the others. Luckily, Duggee had a great idea to bring all the Squirrels together. As a group, they performed the Squirrel Club song about their friends and what theyre good at.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Buggees getting ready, shes gonna walk through all the things people should do from their head right down to their toes.

programme.desc: Catfish is a popular fish in Thailand and today Pearlina is serving it with sticky rice. Before they come over, Pearlinas friends taste one of her important ingredients - lemongrass. Theyre not too keen on it at first, but Pearlina hopes they will like the taste of lemongrass when they taste it in her finished recipe.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how bubbles work when she blows into bubble mixture. She shows how the surface tension of water changes when she adds soap and how to stop bubbles from bursting with her hands, and using her slow-motion camera, she shows how bubbles make a sphere shape.

programme.desc: Kika is a golden labrador and a guide dog for her owner Dr Amit, who is blind, which means he cannot see. She is trained to use her super sight to help Dr Amit do as many things as he can by himself.

programme.desc: The pull of a strange planet splits the Numberblocks into hundreds, tens and units!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Alphablock S loses her letter and cant make the s sound any more. Can she get it back?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends visit a cafe and find George from the CBeebies House there. Meanwhile, Baker Tumble plans a surprise tea party for Mr Tumble.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies enjoy a game of Whats Your Name? Later, theres a surprise when a bowl of tubby custard joins in with the game.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how stinging nettles play an important role in the environment. They find out that a nettles sting can help protect it as well as some insects that shelter on them. They learn that nettles can also be food for some animals too. On the way, they try some nettle tea and pretend to be stinging nettles, trying to keep Ranger Hamzas Mr Fox away!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Elvis refuses to play football after his lucky stinky socks get washed. Evie B leads the hunt for the smelliest smell in the Den so they can make the socks stinky again.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are powered by electricity. Grace rides a very unusual electric motorbike, checks out a solar plane and then drives an eco racer.

programme.title: Dodges Pup School
programme.desc: It is a a busy morning at Pup School, so Miss Collie decides it is best for the class to spend some quiet time doing calm activities. The problem is that all the activities sound very exciting to Dodge. He wants to do them all.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: On Invent Something New Day, Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny and Love Monster realise that sometimes, two heads are better than one.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Octonauts and the Flying Foxes
programme.desc: Rising waters in Brazil lead Tweak and Dashi to help fire ants build a bridge above a river; the Octonauts help fruit bats beat the Australian heat.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When a very sleepy Tommy Brock is hoisted up into the Squirrel Camp, Peter and friends must work out how to get the snoozing brute back down to earth before he wakes up!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It is a rainy day in the Clubhouse which means its a perfect day for a board game. The Squirrels are introduced to Duggees favourite game, Planet Zap - Quest for the Power Orb.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When Sunny doesnt want to see the Sweetwood Band, even though he loves music, Vida helps him out with earmuffs and he gets to enjoy the show.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea tricks Supertato and Broccoli with fake fortune cookies that tell them to go outside the supermarket. Thankfully, Carrot doesnt believe them and saves the day.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs to knock down his shed, and Mojo has the wrecking ball to do it. But first, Mojo and Bo have to get it all the way across town, which is harder than it sounds!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Blueys Fathers Day card has to be perfect but she cant decide what to draw. Mum reminds her about all the fun times she has had with Dad.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nervous about public speaking, Looph builds a robot to deliver a science talk in her place, but her Piripenguin friends are determined to help her do it herself.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo want a lick of each others ice cream. But they are so focused on making sure each lick is fair, they dont notice their ice creams are melting.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: When Dr Ollie discovers the Story Bus is delayed, he vows to save the day by inventing a new story to tell Miss Hoolie and the children.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Its the hottest day of the year and Waffle is eating ice cream to cool down. Outside in the sweltering heat, Simon turns on the sprinkler to help everyone cool down, but poor Waffle doesnt like the sprinkler and darts through the hole in the fence to Mrs Hobbss garden, looking for shade. The Brooklyn-Bells try to coax him back, but he refuses to return. Doug decides to make a mini swimming pool by filling the ball pit with cool water. Waffle loves swimming pools.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafis dads turn into babies, teaching her that being a grown up means acting responsibly.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Its Time to Post a Picture
programme.desc: JoJo has drawn a picture to show Great Gran Gran over a video call, but just as shes about to show it, the internet goes down! Gran Gran suggests they post JoJos picture to Great Gran Gran in Saint Lucia.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and her best friend Duck embark on simple but exciting adventures, exploring the world both near and far in their own wobbly and imaginative way. Duck isnt careful enough with his favourite toy robot.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny, Small and Baby Soup Dragon hear the sound of something approaching from space. They see a large space module hurtling towards the planet. I lands a little way away, a door opens and out steps a strange creature in a space suit!

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa go on the hunt for an intriguing sound.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos are indoors playing with their bricks and they decide to make three towers. Makka Pakka is making piles of stones, but he needs another one to make three piles of three stones. Here comes Upsy Daisy and she has a present for Makka Pakka, a stone which she found in her bed.

programme.title: Hushabye Lullabye
programme.desc: Dillie Dally and the Bedtime Sheep meet Hushabug, the Chumbles, Flutterbye Butterfly and the Hushabirds to do the Snuggly Buggly dance.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp shows us how to create beautiful crafts with items we already own. In this episode, Kirstie turns trash into treasure with Mexican tin art, soap recycling and a tin can dog.

programme.title: Eurosmash! Top 50
programme.desc: The 50 biggest Eurodance smashes of all time. Gala, Gina G, Urban Cookie Collective, Livin Joy, Culture Beat and so many more. Dont miss this!

programme.title: A Decade of Beats! The 90s
programme.desc: Need a fix of 90s club classics? Lock in for a marathon of memories as we take you back to greatest decade of dance! The 90s!

programme.title: 2010s: When Dance Takes Over!
programme.desc: From the era of the Superstar DJ to the deep house revival. This is the definitive 2010s. Weve locked in 50 unstoppable tracks that help shape a generation!

programme.title: A Decade of Dance! The 2010s
programme.desc: We give you heavy hits, vocal anthems, dirty beats and bangin basslines. This is the 2010s at it finest!

programme.title: 90s Belters with Stephanie Hirst!
programme.desc: Join Stephanie Hirst as she spins some awesome belters! From 1990 to 1999 heres the lowdown on her fav 3 tracks of each year.

programme.title: A Decade of Beats! The 90s

programme.title: Forgotten Classics! 2010-2019
programme.desc: Twenty five dance hits from the 2010s that you have probably forgotten. Expect Black Eyed Peas, Swedish House Mafia, Roll Deep and Martin Garrix.

programme.title: 25 Huge Party No.1s!
programme.desc: 25 huge party chart toppers! From Craig David to Swedish House Mafia.

programme.title: Utah Saints: Warehouse Daze Top 30
programme.desc: 30 tracks. One legendary era. Join Utah Saints and experience the early 90s warehouse rave scene with their ultimate setlist. This is for the Old Skool massive!

programme.title: The Old Skool Originals!
programme.desc: Lets go way back with some classic throwback hits, long forgotten beats and some good old fashioned rave anthems!

programme.title: 40 Block Rockin Beats!
programme.desc: Back with another one of those block rockin beats! Heres 40 of the most rampant fuel filled tracks of all time! We have the beats and were not afraid to use them.

programme.title: Take Me To The Clouds Above!
programme.desc: The sound that started it all! Join us for a selection of legendary Clubland classics. Expect high-energy beats, the biggest vocal anthems and feel good party vibe for all!

programme.title: Titanium! 50 Essentials of the 10s
programme.desc: Avicii, Calvin Harris, David Guetta, Clean Bandit, Kygo and so many more from 2010 to 2019. Here is our top 50.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Gets Caught
programme.desc: Phoebe and Rachel see Chandler getting into a car with a mystery woman and follow them to a large house in the suburbs. They tell Monica of their fears and are shocked by the truth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where the Stripper Cries
programme.desc: Joey appears as a celebrity contestant on `The $100,000 Pyramid with its actual host Donny Osmond, but Joeys dubious IQ costs his partner the prize money. Meanwhile, Monica and Rachel hire an ungainly stripper, Roy - without seeing him first - for Phoebes bachelorette party. Also, Chandler and Ross attend a college reunion and meet an attractive woman they both promised not to date during school.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Its Phoebe and Mikes big day, but all does not go to plan. Wedding planner Monica is driving the bride to distraction, New York is hit by its worst blizzard for 20 years, and Joey is taking his role standing in for Phoebes incarcerated father far too seriously. Chandler and Ross are upset that neither of them has a role, until Mike needs to replace one of his groomsmen.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Speaks French
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler discover that their prospective adoptive childs biological father may be a `shovel killer, Ross and Rachel get dangerously close when they go to visit her sick father, and Phoebe struggles to teach Joey French for an audition.

programme.title: Criminally Funny

programme.title: Criminally Funny

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Theyll be travelling from their home in Essex all the way to the Amalfi Coast in Italy, but the first stop on their road trip is the Netherlands, where they try out one of the countrys oldest sports, canal jumping. The action continues when the pair don lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is literally pushed to the edge as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel, and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains.

programme.desc: Theyll be travelling from their home in Essex all the way to the Amalfi Coast in Italy, but the first stop on their road trip is the Netherlands, where they try out one of the countrys oldest sports, canal jumping. The action continues when the pair don lycra for a spot of velodrome track cycling alongside a world champion.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh continue their European adventure in Germany. They head to the city of Frankfurt, where Brad is literally pushed to the edge as he attempts to abseil down the side of a building, facing forwards. The boys also make a stop at a bratwurst-themed hotel, and soak up some local Bavarian culture when they have a go at finger wrestling. The pair finish off their time in Germany with a wet and wild adventure in the mountains.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres Big Show
programme.desc: Featuring music from Emeli Sande, stand up comedy from rising star Joe Lycett, comedy material from Michael himself and Strictly hero Ed Balls plays Celebrity Send To All. And one unsuspecting member of the public will get the surprise of their life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Marie receives a letter from a relative detailing how wonderful her life is, it drives her insane with jealousy and she enlists Debras help in writing a Barone family update.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Debra grudgingly agrees to let him be more involved in the daily decision making, Ray starts grocery shopping for things that he likes, and he chooses where the family will go on its next vacation.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Held captive at Frank and Maries during a snowstorm, Debra, Ray and Amy actually find themselves having a great time until Debra makes a nasty comment to Frank that totally upsets the good mood.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Big Narstie, Ellie Taylor, Frankie Bridge, Harry Judd, Jonathan Ross, Richie Anderson and Vogue Williams try to help the contestants answer their way to a fortune.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler And Monicas Wedding - Part 1
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler prepare for their wedding. Meanwhile, Joey is starring in a movie with a prestigious actor with a spitting problem.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler And Monicas Wedding - Part 2
programme.desc: Joey encounters problems trying to get off the movie set to officiate the wedding when the star turns up drunk.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica and Chandlers wedding reception is almost upstaged by the news that someone might be pregnant. Meanwhile, Ross tries to impress a pretty single woman, Chandler has troubles on the dance floor, and Joey hopes to impress Chandlers mums date - an important Broadway producer - with his acting ability.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Red Sweater
programme.desc: The girls speculate as to whos the father of Rachels baby, the guys scramble after Chandler loses the film from his wedding, and Monica wants to open the wedding gifts.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everybody seems to know who the father of Rachels baby is except the man himself. Meanwhile, Phoebe and Joey lie about a gas leak to get into Monica and Chandlers apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Rachels attempt to explain how they wound up in bed together quickly descends into an argument over who seduced who, until Ross shocks the room by announcing that he has the whole encounter on video. Meanwhile, Monica and Chandler return from their honeymoon, excited at the prospect of socialising with another just-married couple they met on the flight home.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross gets upset when Rachel goes out on a date with a handsome actor, Phoebe tries to prevent a chef from being sacked, and Chandler kills a co-workers promotion.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Halloween Party
programme.desc: At Monicas Halloween party, Phoebe meets the friendly fiance of her flighty twin Ursula, but becomes alarmed when she learns that her sister has misrepresented herself to her trusting future in-law.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross learns that a pregnant Rachel wants to move out and find a place of her own, so he suggests a vacancy in his building. Then he learns that the elderly occupant is not quite dead yet. Meanwhile, Phoebe is ecstatic when Eric dumps her twin sister Ursula and chooses her instead, and Monica comes unhinged when she suspects that her new maid is stealing her jeans, among other things.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Feeling guilty for denying Chandler a bachelor party, Monica hires a stripper to perform for him, without learning that shes a prostitute. Elsewhere, Rachels father learns about her pregnancy and plans to give Ross a piece of his mind.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An old high-school friend of Monicas is invited to the Thanksgiving dinner with the gang, but Monica is unaware that he isnt exactly fond of Rachel. Meanwhile, Chandler and Phoebe pretend to be mesmerised by American football in order to avoid helping Monica in the kitchen, and Joey vows to devour the entire turkey single-handedly.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler is aghast when Monica buys painfully expensive boots, and Phoebe tries to capitalise on her discovery that Ross son attends school with the son of rock star Sting.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross Step Forward
programme.desc: Ross new girlfriend wants to put their photograph on Christmas cards, Rachel eyes Joey as a possible romantic partner, and the newlyweds cope with Chandlers boss as he goes through a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Dates Rachel
programme.desc: To prepare Joey for his big date the following night, Rachel eagerly agrees to go out with him for a rare night on the town. However, when each is pleasantly surprised with their newfound compatibility, they begin to regard each other in a way that no one could have predicted.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Takes a Bath
programme.desc: As Joey agonises over his secret affection for Rachel, Monica mistakenly thinks that he is infatuated with Phoebe. Meanwhile, Ross and Rachel promise not to try to find out the sex of their unborn child, but Rachel cant resist having a peek inside the doctors confidential folder. Also, Monica urges a reluctant Chandler to enjoy the soothing pleasures of a scented bath, but realises that shes created a tub monster.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Secret Closet
programme.desc: Joey rushes pregnant Rachel to hospital when she experiences pains. When Ross feels left out, Joey makes a gallant suggestion. Meanwhile, Chandler is surprised to discover what is behind the locked door of Monicas secret cupboard, and Phoebe gets the impression that Monica is enjoying one of her massages a little too much.

programme.title: Con Air
programme.desc: Set aboard a federal aeroplane transporting convicts to a maximum security facility. One passenger, due for release on parole after eight years in prison, is about to discover that his flight home will turn into a disaster when the prisoners hijack the aircraft. In a race against time, he joins forces with the US Marshal to prevent the criminals from blowing the plane and all on board to pieces.

programme.title: Jack Whitehall: Live at the Hammersmith Apollo
programme.desc: From dates at Nandos to lads holidays in Magaluf, British customer service etiquette to having sex at his parents house. Jack dances from one story to another. Recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts this skit comedy show where the actors on the show, usually Wayne Brady, Colin Mochrie, Ryan Stiles and another guest star or two do different comedy skits. Its all improv and made up on the spot.

programme.desc: Law enforcement officers pursue fugitives on the run. A man wanted for carjacking runs out of road during a high-speed chase; an officer climbs on the hood of a car while trying to prevent a fugitive from fleeing.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A look at some of the strangest arrests from across the country: An allegedly intoxicated woman suggests cops lock her up. An uncle enters the wrestling ring to defend his nephews honour. While shopping, a man places items up his kilt.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A naked man shows up unexpectedly on a homeowners doorstep. A jeweller locks a woman in a vault to wait for police to arrive. And police use a kids bike with training wheels to chase a suspect.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A look at some of the wildest courtroom moments. A man accused of indecent exposure slams his head against a courtroom desk during questioning, a father takes his 19-year-old daughter to court over back rent; a woman in court for a bond violation.

programme.desc: The story of 17-year-old Thomas Griffiths, who killed his girlfriend and arranged the crime scene to look like a suicide after she broke up with him.

programme.sub-title: The Mystery Of Missing Margaret Fleming

programme.title: Murder at My Door With Kym Marsh
programme.desc: Susan Annis was a dedicated nurse, but tragedy struck when she fell into a coma in her room after inviting Kevin Cobb, another nurse shed met and befriended, in for a drink.

programme.desc: The Shotgun Slayer Manhunt: Step into fear as Pittsburgh detectives hunt a brutal killer whose late night attacks leave the city gripped by terror.

programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike buys his newest ever car to create a bespoke Scooby BRZ STI. But hopes of a profit are hit with a hefty bill for the turbo and a new exhaust.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Months after the Northern California expedition, Bryce, Mireya, and Russell head to Washington after a fresh lead points to a target zone where Bigfoot groups are congregating for an unknown reason. Survivalist Biko Wright joins the hunt.

programme.title: Border Control: Spain
programme.desc: A passenger from Albania is being held at Barcelona El Prat Airport. The man is quite annoyed by the checks, but that doesnt bother the customs guards.

programme.title: Broken Lance
programme.desc: Tensions erupt within an Arizona cattle barons household when his three sons vie for control of the ranch.

programme.title: The Bedford Incident
programme.desc: Thriller about a photojournalist on board a destroyer, the Bedford, commanded by Captain Finlander. When the ship discovers a Soviet sub in Greenlands territorial waters, the Captain begins a relentless pursuit that pushes his crew to breaking point.

programme.title: Hell and High Water
programme.desc: Espionage thriller about a plot to make America appear responsible for a communist nuclear strike on North Korea. A submarine commander leads a secret mission to the Arctic to check reports that Russia is assembling a nuclear arsenal on a remote island. The film is so virulently anti-communist that even a British critic called it a "disgrace and an insult".

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone
programme.desc: A lone rider arrives in a small town and meets a young boy who, while awaiting trial for the murder of the local bully, fears he may be killed by his victims family. As he becomes involved in the morass of shifting partnerships that dominate the community, the rider vows to fight against the three corrupt brothers who are intent on taking the law into their own violent hands.

programme.title: The Monuments Men
programme.desc: During World War II, a group of art scholars and other experts infiltrates enemy territory to retrieve stolen masterpieces before the Nazis destroy them.

programme.title: Kingsman: The Golden Circle
programme.desc: With their headquarters destroyed and the world held hostage, members of Kingsman join forces with their American counterparts to battle a ruthless and common enemy.

programme.title: Polite Society
programme.desc: A martial artist-in-training believes she must save her older sister from her impending marriage. After enlisting the help of her friends, she tries to pull off the most ambitious of all wedding heists in the name of independence and sisterhood.

programme.desc: In 1990s Scotland, a group of Catholic school girls get an opportunity to go into Edinburgh for a choir competition, but theyre more interested in drinking and partying than winning the competition.

programme.desc: A priest and three convicts join forces to evacuate children from a volcano-ravaged South Seas island.

programme.title: Sea Wife
programme.desc: During the evacuation of Singapore, refugees are loaded onto a ship that is later torpedoed by the Japanese. Four survivors, three males and one female, find themselves stuck together in a lifeboat. Back in Britain, one of the males searches for the whereabouts of the female survivor.

programme.title: The Virginian
programme.desc: A ranch foreman falls out with an old friend over a woman and is then involved in hunting him down when he turns to crime. The Virginian wins the battle for the affections of Molly Wood, and Steve responds to losing in love by aligning himself with a gang of cattle rustlers. The Virginian looks on while a posse catch Steve and hang him, and guilt-ridden, he swears to go after the gang-leader.

programme.title: Crash Dive
programme.desc: Set aboard the USS Corsair, a submarine on active duty. Against his wishes, Ward Stewart is made executive officer and given just days to prepare. Before boarding he meets schoolteacher Jean Hewlett, and is captivated. He plans romance, unaware that she already has a sweetheart: Dewey Connors, commander of the Corsair and Stewarts boss. Will their rivalry be discovered in time?

programme.title: Jerry and Marge Go Large
programme.desc: Jerry Selbee discovers a mathematical loophole in the Massachusetts lottery, and with the help of his wife, Marge, he wins millions of dollars.

programme.title: Stand by Me
programme.desc: Bittersweet film about a gang of four 12-year-old boys growing up in Oregon in the 1950s. The story is told in flashback years afterwards by one of the boys - now a writer - and revolves around their search through the countryside around their hometown for the body of a missing teenager, thought to have been killed by a train. Adapted from `The Body, a novella by Stephen King.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: In the shadow of a dark internet conspiracy theory, a group of elitists gathers at a remote location to hunt humans for sport. But their master plan is about to be derailed when one of the hunted, Crystal, turns the tables on her pursuers.

programme.title: The Hate U Give
programme.desc: Starr Carter is a prep school student who witnesses the fatal shooting of her childhood best friend at the hands of a police officer. Facing pressure from all sides of the community, she must find her voice and decide to stand up for whats right.

programme.desc: Featuring pasties, fresh mackerel, pigeon and peas and clotted cream, all products of his beloved Cornwall.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: This edition includes happy pigs from the Isle of Wight, real cider from Somerset, peppery watercress from Hampshire streams, and Thomas Hardys favourite cheese.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree is speeding things up by giving her favourite comfort food recipes the 16-minute treatment. She starts with a deconstructed chicken pot pie for her daughter, Paige, and whips up shrimp and grits in no time.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Its question time on the ranch and Ree is covering topics ranging from recipes and romance, to fashion and ranch favourites. Her answers include three meat, three bean, three chilli chilli, shrimp mango lettuce cups, and linguine with clam sauce.

programme.title: Jamies Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver travels Britain to find out what makes our food great. Hes searching for new ideas and inspiration to create his own versions of British classics. He kicks off his road-trip close to home in the East End, uncovering his familys roots in the area and tasting classic pie and mash and modern Vietnamese street food, as well as meeting a chef turning humble burgers into gourmet cuisine.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: The Mile End Delis classic chopped liver, Ina makes bratwurst at Prime Meats Restaurant and French flageolet beans.

programme.desc: Oyster pan roast and uni butter crostini at John Dory Oyster Bar, Million Dollar Chicken at the Standard Grill, side car cocktails and a taste-off.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Bread Week and the bakers tackle a signature bake designed to be shared. Then, the bakers must tackle a deceptively simple summer staple in the Technical and to finish there is an ambitious Showstopper.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary opens her garden to host a village fete and shares her brilliant flair for turning easy dishes into spectacular crowd pleasers. Shes visited by two passionate brothers for a lesson in the in the secrets of authentic Italian pizza making.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by top TV chef Monica Galetti. Theyre in Oxford, where Monica discovers the delights of an organic local distiller. James takes a river trip to a fantastic pick your own farm where he picks some raspberries and he uses them to cook a delicious pudding on the banks of the river. James also visits one of the worlds best chefs, Raymond Blanc, at his restaurant Le Manoir aux QuatSaisons, where he enjoys some of Raymonds Michelin-starred dishes.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is in South Wales, accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. James discovers award-winning Welsh wine and cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside. He samples the delights of organic Welsh chocolate and visits cooking legend Shaun Hill at his renowned restaurant The Walnut Tree. The day comes to an end at Barry Island, where James and Stephen enjoy all the fairground rides at this famous resort and James cooks Barry Island churros overlooking the beach.

programme.title: Jamies Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie travels to Yorkshire, uncovering how the Industrial Revolution, and the need for immigrant workers, changed everything from the people on our streets to the food on our plates. In Leeds, home to the third-largest Jewish community in Britain, Jamie samples chicken soup and smoked fish.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina and Giada De Laurentiis cook up a lobster and potato salad lunch, mascarpone and lemon gnocchi, and a chocolate cassis cake.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Chef Tyler Florence and Ina make a chocolate birthday cake for Tylers son and fried chicken with winter slaw.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Dairy Week and Prue and Paul have set some tricky challenges including a Technical that dates back to Henry VIII and a Showstopper usually found at Indian weddings.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: This is indulgence at its best with Marys mouth-watering dishes that give the everyday a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as Mary pursues the history of the Great British pie and tempts us with the secret to an easy sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Michelin-starred chef Gareth Ward. They enjoy some Welsh Mountain cider tasting, then James uses some to dish up some cider- infused apple fritters with cider custard. Gareth discovers a cattle farm which rears Welsh Wagyu cattle who are fed on local beer. Later they take time out and Gareth is challenged to a thrilling rally car ride, while James demonstrates his dog training prowess with his trusty hound Cooper.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: Jamess final stop is in Englands first National Park: The Peak District. He is joined by cooking legend Tessa Bramley, who serves him up a delicious beef fillet along with vegetables from her beautiful garden. James takes part in an unusual contest, hen racing, where he challenges a local lad and world champion to a race. After discovering some award-winning gin, James meets a local forager and samples some of his elderflower wine.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: Featuring free-range geese in Leicestershire, bitter from Southwold, some delicious kippers, and free-range pork from the Suffolk Black.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Rick samples a traditional hotpot and some mouthwatering cheese, and in the Lake District he finds wild boar and the famous Herdwick sheep.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ladd is going ranching in Kansas, so Ree Drummond plans ahead with breakfast burritos packed with an all-in-one spicy meat and egg scramble for him to share with the cowboys.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is hosting a cooking and eating get-together with her gal friends. They team up to cook a great menu of white spinach artichoke lasagna with a butternut and kale salad - two easy dishes perfect for effortless entertaining.

programme.title: Jamies Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie travels to Yorkshire, uncovering how the Industrial Revolution, and the need for immigrant workers, changed everything from the people on our streets to the food on our plates. Jamie starts at the Kings Arms pub in the village of Heath where huge Yorkshire puddings are washed down with pints of ale. In Leeds, home to the third-largest Jewish community in Britain, Jamie samples chicken soup and smoked fish.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina and Giada De Laurentiis cook up a lobster and potato salad lunch, mascarpone and lemon gnocchi, and a chocolate cassis cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its Dairy Week and Prue and Paul have set some tricky challenges including a Technical that dates back to Henry VIII and a Showstopper usually found at Indian weddings.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: This is indulgence at its best with Marys mouth-watering dishes that give the everyday a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as Mary pursues the history of the Great British pie and tempts us with the secret to an easy sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: In Icelands capital city, Paul bakes using volcanic geothermal energy, meets food writer Eva Laufey who introduces him to the countrys unique Skyr yoghurt, and creates two of his own bakes inspired by Iceland.

programme.title: Prue Leiths Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue cooks a range of recipes perfect for entertaining guests before welcoming Alison Hammond into her kitchen as the pair make something sweet together.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits Americas most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam is in Portland, where is going to sample an elegant lobster roll sandwich. Later on, he is given the challenge of eating his way through a seven-pound seafood bounty in one hour.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Adam visits a joint in Oklahoma to try a juicy 22-ounce prime rib. Later on, he takes on the challenge of eating five burger patties topped with American cheese and tortilla chips.
