programme.desc: Nick and the team head to Warrington help adapt the home of Dave McCartney, who suffered a massive stroke and is now forced to sleep in a hospital bed in the kitchen.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back with him to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: The team investigate a Scottish millionaire found dead in his own shark tank, leading Annika to reflect on Jekyll and Hyde.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews attend a man who has fallen off his bike and a 4-year-old struggling with tonsillitis. Plus, a 35-year-old man with chest pains proves very hard to find.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics treat a pregnant woman who has fallen on her baby bump. Plus, paramedics are called to an unconscious child with a complex medical condition.

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers speaks to Erin Caffey, who masterminded the brutal murder of her family when aged just 16.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and her gang, organiser Dilly, carpenter Rob and cleaner Iwan, challenge the Dowling family to sort their life out in seven days, and in order to do this, they must embark on a mammoth declutter. Its the most cluttered house they have seen so far, and Stacey asks the family to try to let go of at least half their possessions, so that they can then beautifully reorganise their home.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis spends time with Canadian comedian, actor and writer Katherine Ryan. Since her arrival in the UK 15 years ago, Katherine has become one of the countrys most outspoken performers.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Lying off the North Sea is the unassuming Suffolk village of Orford. Its behind a large shingle spit called Orford Ness. But as archaeologist Ben Robinson unearths, its been at the forefront of defending the British Isles for over 800 years. Today its imposing castle built by Henry II is being conserved by English Heritage. Curator Dr Shelley Garland explains the process to Ben and how theyve devised the best way to preserve its crumbling stonework.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores Parkgate, on the estuary of the River Dee in Cheshire. Today the village is landlocked, cut off from the Dee and the Irish Sea due to the estuarys shifting silt. But 250 years ago, it was a bustling harbour and as Ben finds out from Dr Gillian OBrian, Parkgate was a gateway for thousands of Irish immigrants from the 1620s onwards.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team set out on a Pre-War challenge with a 1939 three wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team invest in a piece of French art this week- a 1971 Citroen DS21 Pallas.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Looking at the end of the war, and at how the superpowers confronted each other to establish a new political order.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them In The Fields
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragons teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Ascot Racecourse, where the Pavilion is full of people hoping for a high valuation of their old treasures. Among the objects valued are the first ever document copying machine, dating back over 200 years, and a model of a champion racehorse. Finally, a small coin dug up by a young visitor brings with it a surprising valuation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts are kept busy with another series of exciting finds, including a gold bangle set with precious stones that was found at the bottom of a water tank. An early valentine tells the story of an unrequited love affair.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek, with help from the Mathewson clan, drag a mini pickup out of the barn where its been for 20 years, and a 350cc Douglas Mark V motorbike from 1950 goes under the hammer.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Four VW camper vans pitch up at the auction house looking for a new home and Derek picks up an Austin 16 that goes like stink and has some unusual modern modifications.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Looking at the end of the war, and at how the superpowers confronted each other to establish a new political order.

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: Studying the seasonal changes in the richest waters on Earth, where the annual cycle of the Sun drives an explosion of life, reveals an extraordinary variety of marine animals including seals, dolphins, jellyfish and the bizarre walking handfish.

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: The fragile ecosystems that have grown around the worlds coral reefs. The reefs take hundreds of years to develop and are adorned with spectacularly colourful life. However, this is not always an idyllic tropical paradise - one big storm can threaten the entire community.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them In The Fields
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragons teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to North Yorkshire to pick up a veteran London-to-Brighton car, theres an emotional farewell to a 1980s Bedford HA van, and a Toyota Corona finds a new home.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek has to extract a vintage car from its bizarre resting place, while Charlie picks up a Vauxhall Viva from a man who has owned it for over half a century.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the extraordinary ways in which animals battle to survive the elements in a world of extremes. These remote places are rarely visited, yet the animals that survive there are some of the most resilient on Earth.

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: From the frozen poles to the searing deserts, this is an exploration of how animals have come up with strategies to survive the uneven amounts of sunlight that fall on Earth.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory hunt for neglected cars they can fix and flip for a profit. Any losses incurred by their flipping come out of their own pockets.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien go military: Simon converts tank parts to garden furniture whilst Henry takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard.

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Faced with ominous weather warnings, Sandy Watt must decide whether to brave violent storms or head for the safety of the harbour. Two much larger boats, the trawlers Starlight and Starlight Rays, hunt as a pair. Their skippers and crews must have complete trust in each others skill and judgement - when casting their nets on perilous seas the trawlers come within a stones throw of each other.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien go military: Simon converts tank parts to garden furniture whilst Henry takes his life in his hands with a motorised skateboard.

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Faced with ominous weather warnings, Sandy Watt must decide whether to brave violent storms or head for the safety of the harbour. Two much larger boats, the trawlers Starlight and Starlight Rays, hunt as a pair. Their skippers and crews must have complete trust in each others skill and judgement - when casting their nets on perilous seas the trawlers come within a stones throw of each other.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a scenic, thousand-mile rail journey from the Swedish capital, Stockholm, to Abisko in the northern reaches of the Arctic Circle. Steered by his 1936 edition of Bradshaws Continental Railway Guide, Michael boards a steam train to celebrate Midsummer in Marielund, learns to decorate a Dala horse in Mora and takes an icy dip in one of the countrys 96,000 lakes.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how the trial of one `witch sparked Britains witch-hunt craze. She begins her investigation in North Berwick, a seaside town not far from Edinburgh, where the witch hunting craze began. The story goes that in 1590, a coven of witches gathered here to cast a spell to try to kill the King of Scotland, James VI.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Derek picks out a super low mileage MGC and the team pays a little too much to secure it.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: Does one fewer wheel mean cheaper bills? Thats the question the team want to find out this week as they take on a three wheeled wonder - the Reliant Robin.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: As peace descends in 1945, the series ends with a sombre reminder of the human cost of the bloodiest conflict in history.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them In The Air
programme.desc: RAF Kenley is one of the most intact Battle of Britain airfields. What gave Britain the edge during WWII was an integrated defence system and RADAR.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in Nottinghamshire meeting the people of Southwell in the magnificent Minster. Objects exciting the experts include a 17th-century love token of a betrothal box which bursts with secret compartments, and a rare haul of silver that has been gathering dust in an attic. Also, the original speaking clock comes out of retirement.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts gather amidst the beautiful interior of Southwell Minster in Nottinghamshire. Its a thrilling day for the team as the earliest toy train in the programmes history emerges early on, swiftly followed by a pocketwatch made by Britains finest watchmaker, but the real show-stopper is a romantic painting with a handsome valuation.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek auctions off a Jensen Interceptor and a Jensen Healey along with a one-off Wood and Pickett Triumph Dolomite Sprint. Sarah takes a shine to a four-tonne road roller that needs a new home.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1971 Mercedes SL and a 1989 Jaguar XJS emerge from a barn on a chicken farm, a Roller and a Rover go under the hammer and a 1966 Triumph Spitfire is restored to near perfection.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: As peace descends in 1945, the series ends with a sombre reminder of the human cost of the bloodiest conflict in history.

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: How do the tides affect marine life? Find out with the giant stingrays that glide on currents, the snails that surf the waves in pursuit of a meal, and the dolphins that play a deadly game of catch.

programme.title: The Blue Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough explains how animals from in and around the sea manage to make use of the constantly changing areas where land meets water. Spectacular mass turtle egg-layings, which take place in the same location over just a few days each year, and killer whales hunting seal pups are among the unforgettable visual highlights.

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.sub-title: We Shall Fight Them In The Air
programme.desc: RAF Kenley is one of the most intact Battle of Britain airfields. What gave Britain the edge during WWII was an integrated defence system and RADAR.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: The truckers are in Germany to pick up a maintenance machine so big that it has to be loaded onto two trucks, and Eric and Chris go to Dorset to pick up a heavy diesel locomotive.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: Scalloper Golden Promise is pushing force-6 winds to get to newly opening scallops beds ahead of his rivals. With the boat first to the grounds, they are in for a big pay day - but only once they cover the £8,000 fuel expenses to get here. Family-owned trawler Adenia leaves Lerwick aiming to land 1,000 tonnes of mackerel in Norway in just three days time.

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The ways in which wild animals have adapted to survive in human population centres, from cobras hunting in houses to macaques stealing mobile phones to barter with.

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: A look at how the planets limited fresh water is distributed around the globe thanks to storms, and how animals manage in the driest habitats on Earth.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: After missing out on an Austin Maestro, Gus and Will set their sights on another car on the sellers driveway. But can they convince him to do a deal?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in the Cotswolds competing to see who can create the most profit on two old lamps, and they take a spin in a Devonshire fire engine.

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The young crew of the prawn trawler New Dawn are recovering from the devastating loss of one of their shipmates at sea. Elsewhere, on the pair-trawler Sunrise there is a Shakespearean struggle between rival brothers as to who will inherit the wheelhouse from their father.
